rules.name: Hardcoded API keys/secrets
catches: Hardcoded API keys, secrets, tokens, or passwords in source files
AWS keys, Stripe keys, database credentials embedded in code
Private keys stored directly in variables
fix: Move all secrets to environment variables using process.env
Use a secrets manager like AWS Secrets Manager or HashiCorp Vault
Never commit .env files to version control

recommendation.title: Remove hardcoded secrets
recommendation.description: API keys and secrets should be stored in environment variables, not in source code.

rules.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_ on secret variables
catches: NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix on secret environment variables
Private keys, API secrets, or tokens exposed to client-side code
fix: Remove NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix from any secret variables
Use server-side only environment variables for sensitive data

recommendation.title: Remove NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix from secret variables
recommendation.description: Variables with NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix are exposed to the browser. Use them only for public config.

params.message: Secret variable exposed to client with NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix

rules.name: Logging sensitive data
catches: Logging passwords, tokens, API keys, or other secrets
Logging PII like emails, phone numbers, or addresses
Logging full request bodies that may contain sensitive data
fix: Use structured logging that excludes sensitive fields
Never log req.body, headers, or authentication tokens

recommendation.title: Remove sensitive data from logs
recommendation.description: Logging sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII). Use redaction or exclude from logs.

params.message: Sensitive data being logged - redact passwords, tokens, PII

rules.name: Credentials in URL parameters
catches: API keys or passwords passed in URL query parameters
Credentials visible in browser history and server logs
Tokens or secrets in URL paths
fix: Move credentials to HTTP headers or request body
Use Authorization header with Bearer tokens

recommendation.title: Remove credentials from URL
recommendation.description: Credentials in URL params are visible in browser history and logs. Use headers or body instead.

params.message: Credentials in URL - use headers or body instead

rules.name: Cookie without secure flag
catches: Cookies set without Secure flag (can be sent over HTTP)
Cookies without HttpOnly flag (accessible via JavaScript)
fix: Add HttpOnly flag to prevent XSS access
Add SameSite attribute for CSRF protection

recommendation.title: Add secure flag to cookies
recommendation.description: Cookies with credentials should have Secure and HttpOnly flags set.

params.message: Cookie without Secure and HttpOnly flags - add cookie options

rules.name: Self-signed certificate usage
catches: Environment configured to trust invalid certificates
fix: Use valid SSL certificates from trusted CA
Use Let's Encrypt for free certificates
Never disable certificate verification in production

recommendation.title: Use valid SSL certificates
recommendation.description: Self-signed certificates should not be used in production. Use valid SSL certificates.

params.message: Self-signed certificate or disabled cert validation - use valid certs in production

rules.name: SQL injection via string concatenation
catches: SQL queries built with string concatenation
Template literals with variables in SQL
User input directly in query strings
fix: Use an ORM like Prisma or Drizzle
Never concatenate user input into SQL strings

recommendation.title: Use parameterized queries
recommendation.description: SQL injection risk. Use parameterized queries or an ORM.

patterns.message: Potential SQL injection - string concatenation in query
Potential SQL injection - string concatenation in execute
Potential SQL injection - template literal with variable
Potential SQL injection - pool query with string

rules.name: Command injection via exec/eval
catches: Using exec() or execSync() with string concatenation
Dynamic code execution with user-controlled strings
fix: Avoid exec/eval with user input entirely
Use child_process with array arguments instead of shell strings
Use JSON.parse instead of eval for data

recommendation.title: Avoid exec/eval with user input
recommendation.description: Command injection risk. Avoid shell execution with unsanitized input.

patterns.message: Potential command injection - exec with concatenation
Potential command injection - execSync with concatenation
Potential command injection - eval usage

rules.name: IDOR - missing ownership verification
catches: API endpoints that return data based on ID without verifying ownership
User able to access other users' data by changing IDs
fix: Add ownership verification before data access
Check that req.user.id matches the requested resource owner

recommendation.title: Add ownership verification
recommendation.description: IDOR risk: Verify user ownership before accessing or modifying resources.

patterns.message: Potential IDOR - no ownership verification before data access

rules.name: Missing input validation
catches: User input used directly without validation
fix: Add Zod or Yup validation schemas

recommendation.title: Add input validation
recommendation.description: Validate user inputs using Zod, Yup, or similar libraries.

patterns.message: API endpoint found - validate req.body with Zod or Yup

rules.name: Debug mode enabled in production
catches: Debug mode enabled in production configuration
Verbose error reporting exposed to users
fix: Disable debug mode in production configs

recommendation.title: Disable debug mode in production
recommendation.description: Debug mode should be disabled in production.

patterns.message: Debug mode enabled - disable in production

rules.name: Weak authentication policy
fix: Add password strength validation with zxcvbn
Enforce minimum password length and complexity
Check passwords against common password lists

recommendation.title: Strengthen password policy
recommendation.description: Add password strength validation and requirements.

patterns.message: Password field without strength validation - consider adding zxcvbn

rules.name: Unvalidated URL redirects
catches: Fetching URLs from user input without validation
Server making requests to internal infrastructure
fix: Block internal IP ranges and private addresses
Use URL parser to verify hostname before fetching

recommendation.title: Validate URL before fetching
recommendation.description: SSRF risk: Validate and sanitize URLs before fetching.

patterns.message: Potential SSRF - URL from user input without validation

rules.name: Hardcoded encryption keys
catches: Encryption keys hardcoded in source files
fix: Load encryption keys from environment variables
Use key management services like AWS KMS
Never commit keys to version control

recommendation.title: Remove hardcoded encryption keys
recommendation.description: Encryption keys must be loaded from environment variables, never hardcoded.

patterns.message: Hardcoded encryption key detected - use environment variables
Hardcoded encryption key detected - use environment variables
Hardcoded encryption key detected - use environment variables

rules.name: Unvalidated file uploads
fix: Validate file type using magic numbers, not just extension
Store files outside web root with random names

recommendation.title: Validate file uploads
recommendation.description: File uploads must be validated. Check file type, size, and content.

patterns.message: File upload handler without validation - add file type and size validation

rules.name: Using vulnerable/outdated components
catches: Outdated npm packages with known vulnerabilities
Old versions of libraries with security issues
fix: Update dependencies to latest secure versions
Use Dependabot or Renovate for automated updates

recommendation.title: Update dependencies regularly
recommendation.description: Using outdated dependencies with known vulnerabilities. Run npm audit and update regularly.

catches: Outdated npm packages without vulnerability scanning
fix: Run npm audit in CI/CD pipeline
Set up Dependabot for automated PRs

patterns.message: Dependency scanning found

rules.name: Insufficient security logging
catches: Missing audit trail for sensitive operations
No logging of access control violations
fix: Log all login attempts (success and failure)
Log admin actions and sensitive data access

recommendation.title: Add security logging
recommendation.description: Log security-relevant events including login failures, access control violations, and server errors.

patterns.message: Security event logging detected

rules.name: Missing security headers
fix: Set X-Frame-Options to DENY or SAMEORIGIN

recommendation.title: Add security headers
recommendation.description: Add security headers including Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options.

patterns.message: Security headers found

rules.name: Sensitive headers in response
fix: Use Helmet to strip sensitive headers
Configure server to hide version info

recommendation.title: Remove sensitive headers from response
recommendation.description: Remove headers like X-Powered-By that reveal server information.

patterns.message: Sensitive headers removal found

rules.name: Weak cryptographic cipher
catches: Using DES, RC4, or other deprecated ciphers
fix: Disable weak ciphers like DES and RC4

recommendation.title: Use strong ciphers
recommendation.description: Use strong cryptographic ciphers. Avoid weak ones like DES or RC4.

patterns.message: Weak cipher configuration detected

recommendation.title: Use proper JWT algorithms
recommendation.description: JWT with 'none' algorithm is insecure. Use RS256 or HS256.

patterns.message: JWT library found

rules.name: Potential XSS via innerHTML
fix: Sanitize HTML with DOMPurify before rendering

recommendation.title: Avoid innerHTML
recommendation.description: Using innerHTML can lead to XSS attacks. Use textContent or sanitize input.

patterns.message: XSS sanitization found

rules.name: Regex DoS vulnerability
catches: User input used in regex patterns

recommendation.title: Use safe regex patterns
recommendation.description: User input in regex can lead to ReDoS attacks. Validate and limit input.

patterns.message: Potential ReDoS: nested quantifiers detected

rules.name: Missing Content Security Policy
catches: XSS attacks can load external scripts
fix: Add CSP header with strict policy
Define allowed sources for scripts, styles, images
Start with report-only mode to find issues

recommendation.title: Add Content Security Policy
recommendation.description: CSP helps prevent XSS and data injection attacks.

patterns.message: CSP header found

rules.name: CORS wildcard origin
catches: Any website can access your API
Credentials can be sent to any origin

recommendation.title: Restrict CORS origins
recommendation.description: Using wildcard origin in CORS is insecure. Specify explicit origins.

patterns.message: Insecure CORS wildcard configuration detected

rules.name: Missing X-Frame-Options
catches: Site can be embedded in iframes

recommendation.title: Add X-Frame-Options header
recommendation.description: Prevents clickjacking attacks by controlling iframe embedding.

patterns.message: X-Frame-Options header found

rules.name: Missing HSTS header
catches: Site accessible over HTTP after HTTPS
fix: Add preload flag for browser inclusion

recommendation.title: Enable HSTS
recommendation.description: HTTP Strict Transport Security forces HTTPS connections.

patterns.message: HSTS header found

recommendation.title: Add X-Content-Type-Options
recommendation.description: Prevents MIME type sniffing attacks.

patterns.message: X-Content-Type-Options header found

rules.name: Potential path traversal
fix: Validate path is within expected directory

recommendation.title: Validate file paths
recommendation.description: User input used in file paths can lead to path traversal attacks. Validate and sanitize paths.

patterns.message: Potential path traversal - user input in file path

rules.name: XML External Entity vulnerability
fix: Disable external entities in XML parser

recommendation.title: Disable XML external entities
recommendation.description: XXE attacks can read local files. Disable external entity processing.

patterns.message: XML parsing found

rules.name: Insecure random number generation
catches: Session IDs or tokens generated with Math.random()
Predictable random values used for security
fix: Never use Math.random() for security purposes

recommendation.title: Use secure random generation
recommendation.description: Math.random() is not cryptographically secure. Use crypto.randomBytes() or crypto.randomUUID().

patterns.message: Math.random() is not cryptographically secure - use crypto.randomUUID()

rules.name: Potential prototype pollution
catches: User input merged into objects without sanitization

recommendation.title: Prevent prototype pollution
recommendation.description: User input merged into objects can pollute prototypes. Use safe merge libraries.

patterns.message: Prototype pollution protection found

recommendation.title: Use safe deserialization
recommendation.description: Deserializing untrusted data is dangerous. Use JSON.parse() or safe libraries.

patterns.message: Safe JSON parsing found

recommendation.title: Use parameterized queries
recommendation.description: SQL queries should use parameterized inputs to prevent injection.

patterns.message: Potential SQL injection - use parameterized queries or ORM

rules.name: Potential race condition in financial operations
fix: Use optimistic locking with version field

recommendation.title: Prevent race conditions in financial ops
recommendation.description: Use transactions or locking for operations that modify balances or counts.

patterns.message: Potential race condition - use database transaction for balance/count updates

rules.name: Potential timing attack
fix: Compare hashes instead of raw values

recommendation.title: Use constant-time comparisons
recommendation.description: Variable-time comparisons can leak timing information. Use crypto.timingSafeEqual().

patterns.message: Constant-time comparison found

rules.name: Dependency override vulnerability
fix: Use direct dependencies instead of overrides

recommendation.title: Audit dependency overrides
recommendation.description: Dependency overrides can introduce malicious code. Audit all overrides.

patterns.message: Dependency management found

rules.name: Potential typosquatting attack
catches: Package names that could be typosquatted
fix: Use npm audit to check for issues

recommendation.title: Verify package names
recommendation.description: Check for typosquatting attacks in dependencies. Use exact versions.

patterns.message: Pinned dependency versions found

rules.name: Verbose error information leakage
catches: Raw error messages returned to clients
Database schema details in error messages
fix: Return generic error messages to users

recommendation.title: Sanitize error messages
recommendation.description: Error handlers should not expose raw error messages, stack traces, or internal details to clients.

patterns.message: Raw error object exposed in response - sanitize error messages
Error message directly returned - avoid exposing internal details
Catch block returns raw error - sanitize before sending to client

rules.name: NoSQL injection vulnerability
catches: User input directly in MongoDB queries

recommendation.title: Use parameterized NoSQL queries
recommendation.description: NoSQL queries should not accept user input directly. Use query builders or sanitize operators.

patterns.message: User input directly in NoSQL query - use sanitized query builders
User input directly in NoSQL query - sanitize or use parameterized queries
Raw user input in NoSQL operation - use query builders to prevent injection

rules.name: Timing-based information disclosure
catches: Early return when user not found in login
Different response times for valid vs invalid usernames
fix: Always perform password comparison even if user not found
Return same response time regardless of user existence

recommendation.title: Use constant-time comparisons
recommendation.description: Login and authentication should use constant-time comparisons to prevent username enumeration.

patterns.message: Early return on user existence can leak valid usernames via timing
Check password only after user lookup to prevent timing attacks

rules.name: Type juggling vulnerability
catches: Loose equality that can be bypassed

recommendation.title: Use strict equality
recommendation.description: Use strict equality (===) instead of loose equality (==) in security checks.

patterns.message: Loose equality with password - use strict equality (===)
Loose equality in auth context - use === to prevent type coercion bypass
Loose equality with token - use !== for strict comparison

rules.name: Open redirect vulnerability
catches: Redirecting to URLs from user input
fix: Verify URL is relative or from trusted domain

recommendation.title: Validate redirect URLs
recommendation.description: Redirect URLs must be validated against an allowlist. Never trust user-supplied URLs.

patterns.message: Unvalidated redirect using user input - validate against allowlist
Template literal redirect with user input - validate URLs

recommendation.title: Use secure session storage
recommendation.description: JWTs and tokens should be stored in HttpOnly cookies, not localStorage or sessionStorage.

patterns.message: localStorage used for sensitive data - use HttpOnly cookies instead
sessionStorage used for sensitive data - use HttpOnly cookies instead
Token stored in localStorage - vulnerable to XSS attacks

rules.name: Plaintext password storage
fix: Hash passwords with bcrypt or argon2

recommendation.title: Hash passwords before storage
recommendation.description: Passwords must be hashed using bcrypt, argon2, or scrypt before storing in database.

patterns.message: Password directly from request - ensure hashing before storage
SQL INSERT with plaintext password - use parameterized queries with hashing
Password being stored without hashing - use bcrypt/argon2
Base64 encoding used for password - use proper hashing instead

rules.name: Unprotected attribute injection
catches: User input spread directly into database updates
Users can set admin flags via API
fix: Never allow role/is_admin in user input

recommendation.title: Whitelist allowed fields
recommendation.description: Database update operations should whitelist allowed fields. Never spread user input directly.

patterns.message: Spreading user input in update - whitelist allowed fields
User input spread into model - protect sensitive fields like is_admin, role
Object.assign with user input - whitelist fields to prevent privilege escalation

rules.name: Hardcoded default credentials
catches: Default admin accounts in seed data
fix: Require password change on first login

recommendation.title: Remove default credentials
recommendation.description: Seed scripts and migrations should not contain default admin accounts. Remove before production.

patterns.message: Default admin credentials in code - remove before production
Seed data with admin user - ensure removed in production
Default admin email in seed - verify not deployed to production

rules.name: Weak JWT secret constant
fix: Use 256+ bit random secret from environment

recommendation.title: Use strong JWT secrets
recommendation.description: JWT secrets must be long, random strings loaded from environment variables.

patterns.message: Weak JWT secret detected - use strong random string from environment
Short or predictable JWT secret - use 256+ bit random string

rules.name: Client-side validation only
catches: Only HTML5/form validation without server check
fix: Add server-side validation with Zod or Yup
Validate in API routes before processing

recommendation.title: Add server-side validation
recommendation.description: Client-side validation can be bypassed. Always validate on the server as well.

patterns.message: Validation config found - ensure server-side validation is also implemented

rules.name: Unverified JWT signature
catches: Trusting JWT payload without signature check

recommendation.title: Verify JWT signatures
recommendation.description: JWT tokens must have their signatures verified. Never trust decoded payload without verification.

patterns.message: JWT decoded without verification - use verify() instead of decode()

JWT signature not being verified - always verify with a secret/key

rules.name: Dependency confusion attack risk
catches: npm configured to pull from wrong registry
Malicious package with same name as internal
fix: Configure npm to use private registry
Use scoped packages for internal code

recommendation.title: Secure package resolution
recommendation.description: Configure npm/pnpm to prefer private registries over public for internal packages.

patterns.message: .npmrc found - verify registry configuration to prevent dependency confusion

rules.name: Unicode normalization vulnerability
catches: Unicode characters not normalized in usernames
Different Unicode chars that look the same

recommendation.title: Normalize Unicode strings
recommendation.description: Account recovery should normalize Unicode characters to prevent homograph attacks.

patterns.message: Case-insensitive comparison without Unicode normalization

rules.name: Dangerous eval usage
fix: Never use eval with user input

recommendation.title: Avoid eval with user input
recommendation.description: eval(), new Function(), and similar functions with user input lead to RCE vulnerabilities.

patterns.message: eval() usage detected - can lead to RCE if input is user-controlled
new Function() usage - avoid dynamic code execution

rules.name: Insecure temporary file creation
fix: Use OS temp directory with secure permissions

recommendation.title: Use secure temp file handling
recommendation.description: Temporary files should be created with secure random names in secure directories.

recommendation.title: Avoid dynamic imports with user input
recommendation.description: Dynamic import or require with user input can lead to RCE.

recommendation.title: Block .git directory access

recommendation.description: Cryptographic signing and token generation should happen server-side, not in frontend.
